syntax:
## HTTP / REST API Cheat Sheet

### HTTP Methods
| Method | Purpose | Safe? | Idempotent? |
|--------|---------|-------|-------------|
| GET | Read resource | ✓ | ✓ |
| POST | Create resource | ✗ | ✗ |
| PUT | Replace resource | ✗ | ✓ |
| PATCH | Update resource | ✗ | ✗ |
| DELETE | Delete resource | ✗ | ✓ |

### Status Code Groups
- **2xx Success**: 200 OK, 201 Created, 204 No Content
- **3xx Redirect**: 301 Moved, 302 Temporary Redirect
- **4xx Client Error**: 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 409 Conflict, 422 Unprocessable
- **5xx Server Error**: 500 Internal Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable

### REST URL Conventions
```
GET    /api/users          → list all users
POST   /api/users          → create a user
GET    /api/users/42       → get user 42
PUT    /api/users/42       → replace user 42
PATCH  /api/users/42       → update user 42 fields
DELETE /api/users/42       → delete user 42
GET    /api/users/42/posts → user 42's posts
```

### Request with fetch (JavaScript)
```javascript
// GET
const data = await fetch('/api/users').then(r => r.json());

// POST
const res = await fetch('/api/users', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json',
             'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token },
  body: JSON.stringify({ name: 'Alice', email: 'a@b.com' })
});
```

commonErrors.description: Calling variables outside their declared scope.

interviewNotes: Be ready to explain scoping rules and time/space characteristics.
bestPractices: Use const / final for read-only variables.
memoryTricks: Think of scopes as Russian nesting dolls — inside dolls can see outside, but outside cannot see inside.
quickRevision: Variables store data. Loops repeat blocks. Functions encapsulate tasks. Objects bundle states.
